bfp.config.yacl_not_found.description: BFP uses the configuration library "YetAnotherConfigLib" to generate the in-game configuration screen. It is not a required dependency, but it must be installed if you would like to adjust the configuration while in-game. Otherwise you may modify the configuration manually in .minecraft/config/bfp.json
bfp.config.category.quick.name: Quick Settings
bfp.config.category.quick.tooltip: The most important settings. Click 'Advanced Settings' for the full list.
bfp.config.button.advanced.name: ⚙️ Advanced Settings
bfp.config.button.advanced.description: Opens the complete list of configuration options for rendering, animations, blocks, and physics.
bfp.config.category.general.name: General
Settings for first person player animations
bfp.config.option.enable_first_person_re: Whether BFP's first person player animations should be used or not. Unlike most other animations provided, this one has its own separate renderer that completely overwrites Vanilla's first person renderer. Disable this to show Vanilla's first person player renderer, or for compatibility with mods that integrate into Vanilla's first person renderer.
bfp.config.group.general_positioning.des: Core rendering settings and precise spatial offsets for the first-person arm model.
bfp.config.group.animation_dynamics.name: Animation Dynamics
bfp.config.group.animation_dynamics.desc: Settings that control the speed, intensity, and procedural mechanics of various player actions.
bfp.config.group.first_person_camera_sha: Settings for adjusting how intense the camera shake animations are during specific actions.
bfp.config.option.first_person_camera_sh: Controls the intensity of all camera shake animations.
Controls the intensity of camera shaking from the movement animations, including view bobbing.
Controls the intensity of camera shaking from the item interaction animations, including attacking and mining.
bfp.config.group.first_person_arm_camera: Settings for adjusting the realistic physical drag and momentum of the arms when panning the camera.
bfp.config.option.enable_first_person_ar: Whether the first person arm dampening is enabled or not. Disabling this makes the arms not move at all when moving the camera.
bfp.config.option.first_person_arm_camer: Controls how much friction the spring animation has. The higher the damping, the less it bounces back and forth
Controls how quickly the arms rotate to match up with the camera's rotation
bfp.config.option.first_person_mining_sp: Adjusts how fast the mining animations play. 1 is normal speed, 0.5 is half as fast, and 2 is twice as fast
bfp.config.option.running_arm_swing_inte: Adjusts much the arms will swing during running animations. With a value of 0, the animation will be the same as the walking animation. With a value of 1, the full arm swing animation will play instead of the walking animation
bfp.config.group.first_person_map.name: Map Items
bfp.config.group.first_person_map.descri: Settings for how map items are displayed and animated.
bfp.config.option.first_person_map_movem: Controls the intensity of movement animations when holding a map item. A lower intensity helps with map readability.
bfp.config.group.first_person_arm_offset: Settings for adjusting the position of the first person arms
bfp.config.option.first_person_arm_offse: Arm offset on the X (horizontal) axis
Arm offset on the Y (vertical) axis
Arm offset on the Z (depth) axis
bfp.config.category.combat_weapons.name: Combat & Weapons
bfp.config.category.combat_weapons.toolt: Settings specific to BFP's dynamic combat system and procedural weapon handling.
bfp.config.group.dynamic_combat.name: Cinematic & Dynamic Combat
bfp.config.group.dynamic_combat.descript: Core toggles for immersive weapon mechanics.
Enables a cinematic filter that heavily dampens the camera and amplifies weapon momentum during combat.
Simulates realistic weight. Heavy weapons (like axes) sway slower and have more momentum than light items (like swords).
bfp.config.group.combat_sway_modifiers.d: Fine-tune the procedural weapon sway during actions.
bfp.config.option.combat_sway_multiplier: Master multiplier for all combat-related weapon sway.
bfp.config.option.heavy_weapon_penalty.d: Increases drag and sway penalty specifically for heavy items.
bfp.config.option.light_weapon_bonus.des: Increases responsiveness specifically for light weapons.
bfp.config.group.camera_overhaul.name: Camera Overhaul Physics
bfp.config.group.camera_overhaul.descrip: Cinematic camera tilting and rolling based on player velocity, turning, and strafing.
bfp.config.option.enable_camera_overhaul: Master toggle for all velocity-based camera physics (strafe roll, turning roll, fall pitch, etc).
bfp.config.option.co_strafing_roll.name: Strafe Roll Factor
bfp.config.option.co_strafing_roll.descr: How much the camera tilts when strafing left or right. Higher values create a more aggressive lean into the movement.
bfp.config.option.co_forward_pitch.name: Forward Velocity Pitch
bfp.config.option.co_forward_pitch.descr: How much the camera pitches based on forward/backward acceleration. Simulates the feeling of inertia when sprinting or stopping.
bfp.config.option.co_vertical_pitch.name: Vertical Velocity Pitch
bfp.config.option.co_vertical_pitch.desc: How much the camera tilts up/down when jumping or falling. Creates the sensation of gravity pulling on the player's head.
bfp.config.option.co_turning_roll_intens: How far the camera rolls when turning the mouse quickly. Creates a cinematic banking effect.
bfp.config.option.co_turning_roll_smooth: How quickly the turning roll decays back to center. Higher values make the roll fade faster.
bfp.config.option.co_horizontal_smoothin: Smoothing factor for strafing and forward movement camera effects. Higher values make transitions more gradual.
Smoothing factor for jump/fall camera pitch effects. Higher values make transitions more gradual.
bfp.config.category.block_entities.name: Block Entities
bfp.config.category.block_entities.toolt: Settings for various block entity animations
bfp.config.group.individual_block_entity: Toggles for each type of block entity
bfp.config.group.block_entity_optimizati: Settings for block entity animations that directly affect client performance.
bfp.config.option.block_entity_pose_calc: How often block entity animations' poses are evaluated. For once per tick, the animation is calculated once every in-game tick and then linearly interpolated. For every frame, the pose is calculated every frame and not interpolated. Once per tick should generally be more optimized, but with 20FPS interpolated animations.
bfp.config.option.block_entity_animation: How far away from the player animations are played. Outside the range, vanilla animations play instead.
